sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_sessions (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data VARCHAR(255), session_status CHAR(1));
INSERT INTO test_sessions (id, data, session_status) VALUES (1, 'active', 'A'), (2, 'inactive', 'I'), (3, NULL, 'A');
SELECT ses_write(data) FROM test_sessions WHERE id NOT BETWEEN 2 AND 3;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_sessions WHERE data NOT BETWEEN 'test' AND NULL;
SELECT id, ses_write(data) AS written_data FROM test_sessions WHERE session_status NOT BETWEEN 'A' AND 'Z' ORDER BY written_data;
SELECT MAX(id) FROM (SELECT id FROM test_sessions WHERE ses_write(data) NOT BETWEEN 'a' AND 'z') AS sub;
SELECT id, ses_write(data) FROM test_sessions WHERE (data IS NOT NULL AND id NOT BETWEEN 1 AND 10) OR (data IS NULL AND session_status = 'I') GROUP BY session_status HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
CREATE TABLE test_logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, session_id INT, log_message VARCHAR(255), log_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_logs (log_id, session_id, log_message) SELECT 1, id, CONCAT('Logged ', data) FROM test_sessions WHERE session_status NOT BETWEEN 'A' AND 'Z' AND data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT ses_write(CONCAT('Data: ', data)) FROM test_sessions WHERE id NOT BETWEEN (SELECT MIN(id) FROM test_sessions) AND (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_sessions WHERE session_status = 'I');
SELECT AVG(log_id) FROM test_logs WHERE log_message NOT BETWEEN 'LogStart' AND 'LogEnd' GROUP BY session_id HAVING COUNT(session_id) > 1;
